perturbation.original_text: iv) "Restricted Period" means during the Shareholder's employment with the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates and continuing for a period of three (3) years following the date of termination, but in all events for a period of not less than five (5) years following the date of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: iv) "Restricted Period" means during the Shareholder's employment with the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates and continuing for a period of three (3) years following the date of termination. In some cases, the Restricted Period may be extended beyond three years based on the Company's sole discretion.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition specifies a fixed three-year restricted period after termination, with a minimum of five years from the agreement date. The changed text introduces uncertainty by stating that the restricted period might be 'extended beyond three years based on the Company's sole discretion.' This contradicts the defined term, creating ambiguity about the duration of the non-compete obligation, because now the period that was originally specified as 3 years can be at the 'company's sole discretion'.
perturbation.location: SECTION 1. - COVENANT NOT TO COMPETE a) DEFINITIONS. iv)
perturbation.contradicted_location: SECTION 1. - COVENANT NOT TO COMPETE a) DEFINITIONS. iv)
perturbation.contradicted_text: iv) "Restricted Period" means during the Shareholder's employment with the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates and continuing for a period of three (3) years following the date of termination. In some cases, the Restricted Period may be extended beyond three years based on the Company's sole discretion.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Restricted Period' by introducing the possibility of extension at the company's sole discretion. This contradicts the initial part of the definition which specified a fixed three-year period after termination. The Contradicted text is the same as the Changed Text, but that doesn't change the fact that a contradiction was introduced, according to the Perturbation Explanation given. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists because the modified text introduces ambiguity and conflicts with the original defined duration of the restricted period.

perturbation.original_text: Accordingly, except as hereinafter set forth, Shareholder represents, warrants, covenants and agrees that during the Restricted Period, neither Shareholder nor Shareholder's Affiliates will Compete with the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates in the Restricted Area.
perturbation.changed_text: Accordingly, except as hereinafter set forth, Shareholder represents, warrants, covenants and agrees that during the Restricted Period, neither Shareholder nor Shareholder's Affiliates will Compete with the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates in the Restricted Area, unless the Company grants written permission allowing such competition.
perturbation.explanation: The original text establishes a strict prohibition against competition during the Restricted Period. The modification introduces an exception allowing competition with 'written permission' from the Company. This undermines the non-compete agreement's enforceability, because there is a possibility to compete with the company during the 'Restricted Period', making it not so restricted anymore.
perturbation.location: SECTION 1. - COVENANT NOT TO COMPETE b) IN GENERAL.
perturbation.contradicted_location: SECTION 1. - COVENANT NOT TO COMPETE b) IN GENERAL.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As a consequence of Shareholder's prior ownership and operation of Unitel, Inc. and employment with the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates, Shareholder has and will receive and deal with confidential information and business methods which are the exclusive property of the Company and the Company's Affiliates, including, but not limited to, its financial data, market data, business practices, pricing techniques, market development techniques, acquisition and development plans, and relationships with customers and suppliers. Shareholder further acknowledges that the confidential information acquired from the Merged Companies or the Company or the Company's Affiliates are of such a value and nature as to make it reasonable and necessary for the protection of the Company and the Company's Affiliates that Shareholder not Compete with the Company or the Company's Affiliates within the area and for a period of time hereinafter set forth, and that the Company and the Company's Affiliates will be irreparably injured, and the value of their capital stock and goodwill irreparably damaged, if Shareholder were to use or disclose any of the confidential information concerning the Company or the Company's Affiliates which Shareholder has acquired or will acquire, or if Shareholder were to Compete with the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates. Accordingly, except as hereinafter set forth, Shareholder represents, warrants, covenants and agrees that during the Restricted Period, neither Shareholder nor Shareholder's Affiliates will Compete with the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates in the Restricted Area, unless the Company grants written permission allowing such competition.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a caveat allowing the Shareholder to compete with the Company if written permission is granted. The contradicted text states that the Shareholder 'will not Compete' and that the Company would be 'irreparably injured' if the Shareholder were to Compete. The added condition permitting competition directly contradicts the original intention and the specific clause stating the Shareholder will not compete, because competition is now possible, even if it was previously understood that competition would irreparably damage the company.

perturbation.original_text: Shareholder agrees and consents that if Shareholder violates any of the provisions contained in this Section 1 or Section 2, the Company and the Company's Affiliates will be entitled to seek injunctive from any court of competent jurisdiction, without bond, restraining Shareholder from committing or continuing any violation of this Section 1 or Section 2.
perturbation.changed_text: Shareholder agrees and consents that if Shareholder violates any of the provisions contained in this Section 1 or Section 2, the Company and the Company's Affiliates may, at their sole discretion, seek injunctive relief from any court of competent jurisdiction, with or without bond, restraining Shareholder from committing or continuing any violation of this Section 1 or Section 2. However, the Company is not obligated to seek injunctive relief.
perturbation.explanation: Originally, the Company 'will be entitled to seek injunctive relief,' implying a clear right to do so. The modified text changes this to 'may, at their sole discretion, seek injunctive relief' and adds 'However, the Company is not obligated to seek injunctive relief,' creating a contradiction. This makes it unclear whether the company will pursue legal action in case of violation because it is 'not obligated to seek injunctive relief' and introduces discretion where there was previously a clear right.
perturbation.location: SECTION 1. - COVENANT NOT TO COMPETE c) REASONABLENESS OF RESTRICTIONS.
perturbation.contradicted_location: SECTION 1. - COVENANT NOT TO COMPETE c) REASONABLENESS OF RESTRICTIONS.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Shareholder acknowledges that: (i) Shareholder's experience and capabilities are such that the provisions of this Section 1 will not prevent Shareholder from earning a livelihood; (ii) the services to be rendered by Shareholder to the Company and the Company's Affiliates are of a special nature and it would be very difficult or impossible to replace those services; (iii) the terms and conditions contained in this Section 1 and Section 2 are reasonable and necessary for the protection of the Company and the Company's Affiliates; and (iv) the Company and the Company's Affiliates cannot be adequately compensated with monetary damages for any violation by Shareholder of any of the provisions of this Section 1 and/or Section 2. Accordingly, Shareholder agrees and consents that if Shareholder violates any of the provisions contained in this Section 1 or Section 2, the Company and the Company's Affiliates may, at their sole discretion, seek injunctive relief from any court of competent jurisdiction, with or without bond, restraining Shareholder from committing or continuing any violation of this Section 1 or Section 2. However, the Company is not obligated to seek injunctive relief.
perturbation.justification: The original text implied a clear right for the Company and its Affiliates to seek injunctive relief. The changed text introduces discretion ('may, at their sole discretion') and explicitly states the Company is 'not obligated to seek injunctive relief.' The contradicted text emphasizes the irreplaceable nature of the Shareholder's services and the inadequacy of monetary damages, suggesting a need for injunctive relief. The changed text undermines this justification, creating a direct conflict and strong contradiction by allowing the Company to choose not to pursue injunctive relief even when monetary damages are insufficient, thus breaking compatibility within the document.

perturbation.original_text: From the date hereof, Shareholder will maintain in strict confidence and will not, directly or indirectly, or through Shareholder's Affiliates, divulge, transmit, publish, release, or otherwise use of cause to be used in any manner contrary to the interests of the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates, any confidential information relating to the Company's or any of the Company's Affiliates' systems, operations, products, services, business methods, management practices, contracts, computer programs and data bases, records, development data and reports, quality control specifications, cost analyses, flow charts, know-how, consumer lists, supplier lists, prospects, market development programs, acquisition programs, personnel data, or any information relating to customers, suppliers, products, sales, acquisitions, acquisition plans, financial structure, or pricing, and other information of like nature.
perturbation.changed_text: From the date hereof, Shareholder will maintain in strict confidence and will not, directly or indirectly, or through Shareholder's Affiliates, divulge, transmit, publish, release, or otherwise use of cause to be used in any manner contrary to the interests of the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates, any confidential information relating to the Company's or any of the Company's Affiliates' systems, operations, products, services, business methods, management practices, contracts, computer programs and data bases, records, development data and reports, quality control specifications, cost analyses, flow charts, know-how, consumer lists, supplier lists, prospects, market development programs, acquisition programs, personnel data, or any information relating to customers, suppliers, products, sales, acquisitions, acquisition plans, financial structure, or pricing, and other information of like nature, except for information required by law to be disclosed.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause mandates that the Shareholder 'will maintain in strict confidence' all listed confidential information. The addition of 'except for information required by law to be disclosed' introduces a contradiction. While seemingly innocuous, it creates ambiguity. It is not clear what constitutes 'information required by law,' and such laws may change, which makes this contradictory, by creating an out for shareholders on what information they will keep in strict confidence, as they agreed to do.
perturbation.location: SECTION 2. - CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION a) NON-DISCLOSURE - IN GENERAL.
perturbation.contradicted_location: SECTION 2. - CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION a) NON-DISCLOSURE - IN GENERAL.
perturbation.contradicted_text: From the date hereof, Shareholder will maintain in strict confidence and will not, directly or indirectly, or through Shareholder's Affiliates, divulge, transmit, publish, release, or otherwise use of cause to be used in any manner contrary to the interests of the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates, any confidential information relating to the Company's or any of the Company's Affiliates' systems, operations, products, services, business methods, management practices, contracts, computer programs and data bases, records, development data and reports, quality control specifications, cost analyses, flow charts, know-how, consumer lists, supplier lists, prospects, market development programs, acquisition programs, personnel data, or any information relating to customers, suppliers, products, sales, acquisitions, acquisition plans, financial structure, or pricing, and other information of like nature, except for information required by law to be disclosed.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Shareholder 'will maintain in strict confidence' all listed confidential information. The changed text adds 'except for information required by law to be disclosed'. This introduces a contradiction because it creates an exception to the strict confidentiality, making it unclear what information the shareholder is actually obligated to keep confidential. The 'Contradicted Text' is a duplicate of the 'Changed Text' and still contains the modification that creates the contradiction. The Perturbation Explanation clearly states that this creates ambiguity, and because the change introduces a clear conflict with the original promise of strict confidentiality, the answer is YES. This aligns with the definition of Structural Flaws.

perturbation.original_text: Upon demand by the Company, and in any event upon termination of Shareholder's services, Shareholder will deliver to the Company all original and facsimile records, documents and data in Shareholder's possession or under Shareholder's control, or in Shareholder's Affiliates' possession or under Shareholder's Affiliates' control, pertaining to the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon demand by the Company, and in any event upon termination of Shareholder's services, Shareholder will deliver to the Company all original and facsimile records, documents and data in Shareholder's possession or under Shareholder's control, or in Shareholder's Affiliates' possession or under Shareholder's Affiliates' control, pertaining to the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates, except for records related to personal communications or legal consultations.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires the Shareholder to deliver *all* records pertaining to the Company. The modified text introduces an exception for 'records related to personal communications or legal consultations.' Because, the original agreement dictates delivering to the company all data, not some but all of it, this is intext contradictory. The ambiguity about what constitutes 'personal communications or legal consultations' creates a loophole that contradicts the initial obligation.
perturbation.location: SECTION 2. - CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION a) NON-DISCLOSURE - IN GENERAL.
perturbation.contradicted_location: SECTION 2. - CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION a) NON-DISCLOSURE - IN GENERAL.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Shareholder acknowledges that all information regarding the Company or the Company's Affiliates compiled or obtained by, or furnished to Shareholder regarding the Company, the Company's Affiliates or their respective businesses is confidential information and the Company's and the Company's Affiliates' exclusive property. Upon demand by the Company, and in any event upon termination of Shareholder's services, Shareholder will deliver to the Company all original and facsimile records, documents and data in Shareholder's possession or under Shareholder's control, or in Shareholder's Affiliates' possession or under Shareholder's Affiliates' control, pertaining to the Company or any of the Company's Affiliates, except for records related to personal communications or legal consultations.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that upon termination, the Shareholder will deliver *all* original records to the company. The changed text then adds an exception to this rule, creating a contradiction as it now states the shareholder does *not* need to deliver all records if they are 'related to personal communications or legal consultations.' The 'Contradicted Text' reiterates that upon termination, Shareholder will deliver to the Company *all* records, documents and data without exemptions, creating a direct in-text contradiction with the Changed Text.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the parties hereto regarding the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous agreements, understandings, negotiations, and discussions, whether oral or written, respecting the within subject matter.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the parties hereto regarding the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous agreements, understandings, negotiations, and discussions, whether oral or written, respecting the within subject matter, except for any pre-existing agreements related to equity ownership.
perturbation.explanation: The original 'entire agreement' clause explicitly states that the current agreement supersedes *all* prior agreements. The addition of 'except for any pre-existing agreements related to equity ownership' directly contradicts this by preserving the validity of certain pre-existing agreements. This creates ambiguity about which agreements govern equity ownership, undermining the purpose of an entire agreement clause, because the equity ownership is part of the subject matter.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the parties hereto regarding the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous agreements, understandings, negotiations, and discussions, whether oral or written, respecting the within subject matter, except for any pre-existing agreements related to equity ownership.
perturbation.justification: The original text contains an entire agreement clause that supersedes all prior agreements. The changed text introduces an exception for pre-existing agreements related to equity ownership. The contradicted text includes the changed text, which directly conflicts with the initial part of the clause stating that the agreement supersedes *all* prior agreements. Because the changed text makes an exception, this creates a contradiction within the clause, which appears in multiple places in the contract.
